She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=}} and the consort of {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=}} addressed {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Midori-ku, Saitama|ja|緑区 (さいたま市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-ku, Saitama|qq=}}) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ({{ill|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|ja|清水区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|qq=}}) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to {{ill|Miho no Matsubara|ja|三保の松原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho no Matsubara|qq=}}. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * {{ill|Izumo Daijingu|ja|出雲大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Daijingu|qq=}} ({{ill|Kameoka, Kyoto|ja|亀岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka, Kyoto|qq=}}) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tawaramoto, Nara|ja|田原本町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto, Nara|qq=}}) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shimane)|ja|美保神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shimane)|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue, Shimane|ja|松江市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue, Shimane|qq=}}) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and {{ill|Nunakawahime|ja|沼河比売|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakawahime|qq=}}. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese mythology|qq=}} {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] revision.text:
{{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue [[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * revision.text:
{{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue [[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - A descendant of the shrine’s priests, who became an aerobatic pilot and currently flies for [[Peach Aviation]]. == References == {{Reflist]] == Related Articles == * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Official Website] She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - A descendant of the shrine’s priests, who became an aerobatic pilot and currently flies for [[:en:Peach Aviation|Peach Aviation]]. == References == {{Reflist]] == Related Articles == * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Official Website] ({{lang|en}}) == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Miho-jinja]] * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Miho Shrine Official Website] {{ja icon]] {{Normdaten]] {{shinto-stub]] ==See also== * ==References== {{Commons category|Miho-jinja]] {{Reflist]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub]] {{Beppyo Shrines]] \ {{translated page|ja|三穂津姫命|version=15992750|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines|*Miho Shrine]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsue]] [[Category:History of Matsue]] [[Category:Taisha-zukuri]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Shimane Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecture-designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[sn:Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]] [[zh:三穗津姬]] [[zh:事代主神]] [[ja:三穂津姫命]] [[ja:式内社]] [[ja:国幣中社]] [[ja:別表神社]] [[ja:出雲國神仏霊場]] [[ja:美保神社]] [[ja:神社]] [[ja:えびす]] [[ja:事代主神]] [[ja:大物主神]] [[ja:Izumo Province 風土記]] [[ja:横山氏]] [[ja:出雲大社]] [[ja:神社本庁]] [[ja:大社造]] [[ja:重要文化財]] [[ja:国譲り]] [[ja:大国主]] [[ja:天津神]] [[ja:三穂津姫]] [[ja:松江市]] [[ja:羽衣伝説]] [[ja:御穂神社]] [[ja:青石畳通り]] [[ja:サニー横山]] [[sn:apanese]] [[en:Miho Shrine]] {{Commons category|Miho-jinja}} [[fr:Miho-jinja]] [[zh:美保神社]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - A descendant of the shrine’s priests, who became an aerobatic pilot and currently flies for [[:en:Peach Aviation|Peach Aviation]]. == References == {{Reflist]] == Related Articles == * == External Links == * {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - A descendant of the shrine’s priests, who became an aerobatic pilot and currently flies for [[:en:Peach Aviation|Peach Aviation]]. == References == {{Reflist]] == Related Articles == * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Official Website] ({{lang|en}}) == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Miho-jinja]] * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Miho Shrine Official Website] {{ja icon]] [[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|qq=|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|qq=|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} -
