revision.text:
[[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - A descendant of the shrine’s priests, who became an aerobatic pilot and currently flies for [[:en:Peach Aviation|Peach Aviation]]. == References == {{Reflist]] == Related Articles == * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Official Website] ({{lang|en}}) == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Miho-jinja]] * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Miho Shrine Official Website] {{ja icon]] {{Normdaten]] {{shinto-stub]] ==See also== * ==References== {{Commons category|Miho-jinja]] {{Reflist]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub]] {{Beppyo Shrines]] \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines|*Miho Shrine]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsue]] [[Category:History of Matsue]] [[Category:Taisha-zukuri]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Shimane Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecture-designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[zh:三穗津姬]] [[zh:事代主神]] [[en:Miho Shrine]] {{Commons category|Miho-jinja}} [[fr:Miho-jinja]] [[zh:美保神社]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ja:美保神社]] [[Category:Ja import oversized]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{translated page|ja|三穂津姫}} {{wikidata link|Q6378698}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - A descendant of the shrine’s priests, who became an aerobatic pilot and currently flies for [[:en:Peach Aviation|Peach Aviation]]. == References == {{Reflist]] == Related Articles == * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Official Website] ({{lang|en}}) == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Miho-jinja]] * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Miho Shrine Official Website] {{ja icon]] {{Normdaten]] {{shinto-stub]] ==See also== * ==References== {{Commons category|Miho-jinja]] {{Reflist]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub]] {{Beppyo Shrines]] \ {{draft categories| }} [[zh:三穗津姬]] [[zh:事代主神]] [[en:Miho Shrine]] {{Commons category|Miho-jinja}} [[fr:Miho-jinja]] [[zh:美保神社]] [[ja:美保神社]] {{translated page|ja|三穂津姫}} {{wikidata link|Q6378698}} [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsue]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines|*Miho Shrine]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:History of Matsue]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Ja import oversized]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Prefecture-designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shimane Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha-zukuri]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] [[ja:三穂津姫]][[zh:三穗津姬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | 名 = 三穂津姫 | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 三穂津姫（ミホツヒメ） | 別名 = 三穂津姫尊、御穂津姫命 | 別称 = | 神格 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = 高皇産霊尊 | 兄弟姉妹 = {{Plainlist| * [[オモイカネ|思兼神]] * [[栲幡千千姫命|栲幡千千姫]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] }} | 配偶者 = 大物主神（大国主神） | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[氷川女體神社]] * [[出雲大神宮]] * [[御穂神社]] * [[村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] * [[美保神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[Nihon Shoki]] | 関連氏族 = | image = [[File:Miho-jinja_haiden.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
[[Miho Shrine]] Haiden}} '''Mihotsuhime''' (三穂津姫) is a [[:en:Goddess|goddess]] in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is the daughter of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and the consort of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == Description == Mihotsuhime appears only in an alternative account in the second book of [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the episode of the {{ill|Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacification of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}}. == Nihon Shoki == === Volume Two, Ninth Section, Alternative Account Two === After {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (another name for Ōkuninushi) decided to cede the land and withdrew into the hidden world, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} addressed [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (the *mitama* of Ōnamuchi), saying: "If you take a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} as your wife, it would mean that you still do not completely yield your heart. Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}. Said to be the child of Ōnamochi and [[:en:Nunakawahime|Nunakawahime]]. == External Links == * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8861/ Hikawa Nyotai Shrine - Saitama Prefecture] * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4405062 Miho Shrine - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://izumo-d.org/gosaijin.html Izumo Daijingu Official Website] * [https://murayajinja.com/yuisho/ History of Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine] * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/yuisho/ Miho Shrine - Shrine of Ebisu] [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]] [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == *{{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuniyuzuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (mythology)|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsue City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue City|qq=}} * [[Cultural Properties of Japan#Folk Cultural Properties|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine|Yaegaki Jinja]] * [[:en:Kamosu Shrine|Kamosu Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine|Sada Jinja]] * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Hagoromo Legend|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo Legend|qq=}} * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|qq=}} * [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] * {{ill|Aoshi Ishidatami Street|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoshi Ishidatami Street|qq=}} * {{ill|Sunny Yokoyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunny Yokoyama|qq=}} - A descendant of the shrine’s priests, who became an aerobatic pilot and currently flies for [[:en:Peach Aviation|Peach Aviation]]. == References == {{Reflist]] == Related Articles == * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Official Website] ({{lang|en}}) == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Miho-jinja]] * == External Links == * [http://mihojinja.or.jp/ Miho Shrine Official Website] {{ja icon]] {{Normdaten]] {{shinto-stub]] ==See also== * ==References== {{Commons category|Miho-jinja]] {{Reflist]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub]] {{Beppyo Shrines]] \ [[zh:三穗津姬]] [[zh:事代主神]] [[en:Miho Shrine]] {{Commons category|Miho-jinja}} [[fr:Miho-jinja]] [[zh:美保神社]] [[ja:美保神社]] {{translated page|ja|三穂津姫}} {{wikidata link|Q6378698}} [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsue]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines|*Miho Shrine]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:History of Matsue]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Ja import oversized]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Prefecture-designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shimane Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha-zukuri]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Instead, take my daughter Mihotsuhime as your wife, and lead the eighty myriad deities to eternally protect the imperial descendants." == Etymology == The "-tsu" (ツ) in Mihotsuhime serves as a possessive particle meaning "of," indicating "the princess of Miho." == Shrines dedicated to Mihotsuhime == * {{ill|Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja|氷川女體神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷川女体神社|1=Hikawa Nyotai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549599}} ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori-ku, Saitama]]) - Enshrined together with other deities. * {{ill|Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|ja|御穂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miho Shrine (Shizuoka)|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} ([[:en:Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka|Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka]]) - The main enshrined deity. Located at the entrance to [[:en:Miho no Matsubara|Miho no Matsubara]]. Enshrined alongside {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, who here is also referred to as Mihotsuhiko. * [[:en:Izumo-daijingū|Izumo Daijingu]] ([[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka, Kyoto]]) - Enshrined as the principal deity alongside Ōkuninushi, considered his consort. * {{ill|Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|ja|村屋坐弥冨都比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraya Sumi Mitarai Shrine|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523074}} ([[:en:Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto, Nara]]) - Enshrined as the main deity with Ōmononushi as her consort. * [[:en:Miho Shrine|Miho Shrine (Shimane)]] ([[:en:Matsue|Matsue, Shimane]]) - The main shrine deity. Worshipped together with {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, the son of Ōkuninushi. == See Also == * {{ill|Mihosusumi|ja|ミホススミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosusumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - A deity mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically in the entry for Mihogō of {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052337}}.
